title: India's Cockroach Janta Party parliamentary protest
summary: Youth-led Cockroach Janta Party supporters staged a major march on India's parliament in July 2026 to demand the resignation of the Education Minister over exam paper leaks, which police dispersed using force despite the protest defying a government ban.

events.chapter_summary: On July 20, 2026, thousands of Cockroach Janta Party supporters marched on India's parliament in New Delhi defying a police ban, demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over exam paper leaks, following the forced hospitalization of activist Sonam Wangchuk two days earlier.

The new report specifies that the march occurred on July 20, 2026, coinciding with parliament's monsoon session opening, and characterizes it as one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in India in recent years.
